<think>
We are going to use the sqlgen library to implement the function.
 Steps:
 1. Connect to the database "test_db.sqlite".
 2. Drop the Product table if it exists.
 3. Create the Product table.
 4. Begin a transaction.
 5. Insert the products in batch.
 6. Commit the transaction.
 7. Return true if all operations succeed, false otherwise.

 We note that the sqlgen library uses a fluent interface with the pipe operator.

 We have the following global variables and functions from the reference:
   - sqlgen::sqlite::connect
   - sqlgen::drop<Product> | sqlgen::if_exists
   - sqlgen::write (for batch insertion)
   - sqlgen::begin_transaction
   - sqlgen::commit

 We also note that the operations return Result<Ref<Connection>> and we can chain them with .and_then.

 However, note that the write function in the reference materials is designed to be used in a chain.
 The typical usage is:

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <optional>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <chrono>
#include <iomanip>
#include <sstream>

namespace query_active_users_posts {

struct User {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string username;
};

struct Post {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> post_id;
  int user_id;
  sqlgen::DateTime created_at;
};

struct ActiveUser {
  static constexpr const char* tablename = "ActiveUserView";
  static constexpr bool is_view = true;
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string username;
};

std::vector<ActiveUser> query_active_users_posts() {
    // Connect to the database
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
    if (!conn) {
        throw std::runtime_error("Failed to connect to database");
    }

    // Create User table with if_not_exists
    auto create_user_table = sqlgen::create_table<User> | sqlgen::if_not_exists;
    auto user_table_result = create_user_table(*conn);
    if (!user_table_result) {
        throw std::runtime_error("Failed to create User table");
    }

    // Create Post table with if_not_exists
    auto create_post_table = sqlgen::create_table<Post> | sqlgen::if_not_exists;
    auto post_table_result = create_post_table(*conn);
    if (!post_table_result) {
        throw std::runtime_error("Failed to create Post table");
    }

    // Build the query using sqlgen's fluent interface
    using namespace sqlgen::literals;
    auto start_date = sqlgen::DateTime("2025-12-01");
    auto end_date = sqlgen::DateTime("2025-12-31");

    auto query = sqlgen::select_from<User>("user_id"_c, "username"_c)
                 | sqlgen::inner_join<Post>("user_id"_c == "user_id"_c)
                 | sqlgen::where("created_at"_c >= start_date && "created_at"_c <= end_date);

    // Create the view from the query
    auto create_view = sqlgen::create_as<ActiveUser>(query);
    auto view_result = create_view(*conn);
    if (!view_result) {
        throw std::runtime_error("Failed to create view");
    }

    // Read the results from the view
    auto result = sqlgen::read<std::vector<ActiveUser>>(*conn);
    if (!result) {
        throw std::runtime_error("Failed to read from view");
    }

    return *result;
}

}  // namespace query_active_users_posts

         read_map[item.item_id] = item;
     }

     for (const auto& item : items) {
         auto it = read_map.find(item.item_id);
         if (it == read_map.end()) {
             return false;
         }
         if (it->second.name != item.name || it->second.quantity != item.quantity) {
             return false;
         }
     }

     return true;
   }

   }  // namespace upsert_inventory_items

```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <optional>

namespace analyze_social_network {

struct User {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string username;
  std::string email;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> join_date;
  std::optional<std::string> city;
};

struct Friendship {
  int user1_id;
  int user2_id;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> created_at;
};

struct FriendConnection {
  std::string user1_name;
  std::string user2_name;
};

// Temporary struct for the first query result
struct TempResult {
  std::string user1_name;
  std::string user2_name;
  int mutual_friends_count;
};

std::vector<FriendConnection> analyze_social_network_impl(const std::vector<User>& users, const std::vector<Friendship>& friendships) {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;

  // Connect to the database
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  // Write the User and Friendship data
  sqlgen::write(conn, users);
  sqlgen::write(conn, friendships);

  // Query 1: Mutual friends
  auto query1 = select_from<User>("u1"_t1)("username"_t1, "username"_t2, count("user_id2"_t1))
      | inner_join<Friendship>("f1"_t1)("u1"_t1["user_id"] == "f1"_t1["user_id1"])
      | inner_join<Friendship>("f2"_t1)("f1"_t1["user_id2"] == "f2"_t1["user_id1"])
      | inner_join<User>("u2"_t1)("f2"_t1["user_id2"] == "u2"_t1["user_id"])
      | where(("u1"_t1["user_id"] < "u2"_t1["user_id"]) && exists(select_from<Friendship>("f3"_t1)("u1"_t1["user_id"] == "f3"_t1["user_id1"] && "u2"_t1["user_id"] == "f3"_t1["user_id2"]))
      | group_by("u1"_t1["user_id"], "u2"_t1["user_id"])
      | having(count("f1"_t1["user_id2"]) > 0)
      | to<std::vector<TempResult>>();

  auto result1 = query1(conn);

  // Convert the result to FriendConnection
  std::vector<FriendConnection> final_results;
  for (const auto& res : result1.value()) {
    final_results.push_back(FriendConnection{res.user1_name, res.user2_name});
  }

  // Query 2: Users with more than N friends (N is average)
  auto query2 = select_from<User>("u"_t1)("user_id"_t1, "username"_t1, count("user_id2"_t1).as("friend_count"))
      | left_join<Friendship>("f"_t1)("u"_t1["user_id"] == "f"_t1["user_id1"])
      | group_by("u"_t1["user_id"], "u"_t1["username"])
      | having(count("f"_t1["user_id2"]) > (select_from<Friendship>("f_avg"_t1)(count("*").as("friend_count")) | group_by("user_id1"_t1) | select(avg("friend_count"))));

  auto result2 = query2(conn);

  // Query 3: Friend-of-friend relationships
  auto query3 = select_from<User>("u1"_t1)("username"_t1, "username"_t2)
      | inner_join<Friendship>("f1"_t1)("u1"_t1["user_id"] == "f1"_t1["user_id1"])
      | inner_join<Friendship>("f2"_t1)("f1"_t1["user_id2"] == "f2"_t1["user_id1"])
      | inner_join<User>("u2"_t1)("f2"_t1["user_id2"] == "u2"_t1["user_id"])
      | where(not_exists(select_from<Friendship>("f3"_t1)("u1"_t1["user_id"] == "f3"_t1["user_id1"] && "u2"_t1["user_id"] == "f3"_t1["user_id2"])));

  auto result3 = query3(conn);

  // Query 4: Count friends per user and identify most connected users
  auto query4 = select_from<User>("u"_t1)("user_id"_t1, "username"_t1, count("user_id2"_t1).as("friend_count"))
      | left_join<Friendship>("f"_t1)("u"_t1["user_id"] == "f"_t1["user_id1"])
      | group_by("u"_t1["user_id"], "u"_t1["username"])
      | order_by(count("f"_t1["user_id2"]).desc());

  auto result4 = query4(conn);

  // Query 5: Users in the same city who are not yet friends
  auto query5 = select_from<User>("u1"_t1)("username"_t1, "username"_t2)
      | inner_join<User>("u2"_t1)("u1"_t1["city"] == "u2"_t1["city"] && "u1"_t1["user_id"] < "u2"_t1["user_id"])
      | where(not_exists(select_from<Friendship>("f"_t1)("u1"_t1["user_id"] == "f"_t1["user_id1"] && "u2"_t1["user_id"] == "f"_t1["user_id2"])));

  auto result5 = query5(conn);

  return final_results;
}

}  // namespace analyze_social_network
```

<think>
We are given the task to implement the function `analyze_department_employees_impl` using the sqlgen library.
 The function should:
  1. Connect to the SQLite database "test_db.sqlite"
  2. Create tables for Department and Employee (if they don't exist) and insert the provided data.
  3. Perform four types of joins (inner, left, right, full) between the two tables.
  4. Collect the results of each join into a vector of JoinResult.
  5. Return a JoinAnalysis object containing the four vectors.

 Steps:
  a. Connect to the database.
  b. Use the sqlgen::write function to create tables and insert the data for both Department and Employee.
  c. For each join type, we will use the sqlgen::select_from function to build the query, then execute it and collect the results.

 We note that the reference SQL statements are provided, but we must use the sqlgen library to generate the equivalent queries.

 How to build the joins in sqlgen?
  - We can use the `select_from` function to start a query from one table, then use the `operator|` to add a join.
  - The join types are available as `inner_join`, `left_join`, `right_join`, `full_join` in the sqlgen namespace.

 We are also given the table structures and the JoinResult structure.

 Important: The JoinResult structure uses std::optional because the joins may return NULLs (especially for left, right, and full joins).

 Let's break down the steps in the function:

  1. Connect to the database: `auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");`
  2. Write the Department and Employee data to the database. We can use `sqlgen::write(conn, depts)` and `sqlgen::write(conn, emps)`.
  3. For each join type, we will:
        a. Build the query using `select_from` and the appropriate join.
        b. Execute the query and collect the results into a vector of JoinResult.

 However, note that the provided reference SQL statements are:

   INNER JOIN: 
        SELECT d.department_name, e.first_name, e.last_name, e.salary

        #pragma once

        #include <sqlgen.hpp>
        #include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
        #include <vector>

        namespace analyze_department_employees {

        struct Department {
          sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> department_id;
          std::string department_name;
          std::string location;
        };

        struct Employee {
          sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> employee_id;
          int department_id;
          std::string first_name;
          std::string last_name;
          double salary;
        };

        struct JoinResult {
          std::optional<std::string> department_name;
          std::optional<std::string> first_name;
          std::optional<std::string> last_name;
          std::optional<double> salary;
        };

        struct JoinAnalysis {
          std::vector<JoinResult> inner_join_results;
          std::vector<JoinResult> left_join_results;
          std::vector<JoinResult> right_join_results;
          std::vector<JoinResult> full_join_results;
        };

        JoinAnalysis analyze_department_employees_impl(const std::vector<Department>& depts, const std::vector<Employee>& emps) {
          using namespace sqlgen::literals;

          // Connect to the database
          auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite").value();

          // Write the department and employee data
          sqlgen::write(conn, depts).value();
          sqlgen::write(conn, emps).value();

          // Build the queries
          auto inner_query = select_from<Department>(
              "department_name"_t1.as<"department_name">(), 
              "first_name"_t2.as<"first_name">(), 
              "last_name"_t2.as<"last_name">(), 
              "salary"_t2.as<"salary">()
          )
          | inner_join<Employee>("department_id"_t1 == "department_id"_t2);

          auto left_query = select_from<Employee>(
              "department_name"_t1.as<"department_name">(), 
              "first_name"_t2.as<"first_name">(), 
              "last_name"_t2.as<"last_name">(), 
              "salary"_t2.as<"salary">()
          )
          | left_join<Department>("department_id"_t2 == "department_id"_t1);

          auto right_query = select_from<Employee>(
              "department_name"_t1.as<"department_name">(), 
              "first_name"_t2.as<"first_name">(), 
              "last_name"_t2.as<"last_name">(), 
              "salary"_t2.as<"salary">()
          )
          | right_join<Department>("department_id"_t2 == "department_id"_t1);

          auto full_query = select_from<Employee>(
              "department_name"_t1.as<"department_name">(), 
              "first_name"_t2.as<"first_name">(), 
              "last_name"_t2.as<"last_name">(), 
              "salary"_t2.as<"salary">()
          )
          | full_join<Department>("department_id"_t2 == "department_id"_t1);

          // Execute the queries
          auto inner_results = inner_query(conn).value();
          auto left_results = left_query(conn).value();
          auto right_results = right_query(conn).value();
          auto full_results = full_query(conn).value();

          // Return the analysis
          return JoinAnalysis{
              .inner_join_results = inner_results,
              .left_join_results = left_results,
              .right_join_results = right_results,
              .full_join_results = full_results
          };
        }

        }  // namespace analyze_department_employees

#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace analyze_department_employees {

struct Department {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> department_id;
  std::string department_name;
  std::string location;
};

struct Employee {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> employee_id;
  int department_id;
  std::string first_name;
  std::string last_name;
  double salary;
};

struct JoinResult {
  std::optional<std::string> department_name;
  std::optional<std::string> first_name;
  std::optional<std::string> last_name;
  std::optional<double> salary;
};

struct JoinAnalysis {
  std::vector<JoinResult> inner_join_results;
  std::vector<JoinResult> left_join_results;
  std::vector<JoinResult> right_join_results;
  std::vector<JoinResult> full_join_results;
};

JoinAnalysis analyze_department_employees_impl(const std::vector<Department>& depts, const std::vector<Employee>& emps) {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;

  // Connect to the database
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite").value();

  // Write the department and employee data
  sqlgen::write(conn, depts).value();
  sqlgen::write(conn, emps).value();

  // Build the queries
  auto inner_query = sqlgen::select_from<Department>(
      "department_name"_t1.as<"department_name">(), 
      "first_name"_t2.as<"first_name">(), 
      "last_name"_t2.as<"last_name">(), 
      "salary"_t2.as<"salary">()
  )
  | sqlgen::inner_join<Employee>("department_id"_t1 == "department_id"_t2);

  auto left_query = sqlgen::select_from<Employee>(
      "department_name"_t1.as<"department_name">(), 
      "first_name"_t2.as<"first_name">(), 
      "last_name"_t2.as<"last_name">(), 
      "salary"_t2.as<"salary">()
  )
  | sqlgen::left_join<Department>("department_id"_t2 == "department_id"_t1);

  auto right_query = sqlgen::select_from<Employee>(
      "department_name"_t1.as<"department_name">(), 
      "first_name"_t2.as<"first_name">(), 
      "last_name"_t2.as<"last_name">(), 
      "salary"_t2.as<"salary">()
  )
  | sqlgen::right_join<Department>("department_id"_t2 == "department_id"_t1);

  auto full_query = sqlgen::select_from<Employee>(
      "department_name"_t1.as<"department_name">(), 
      "first_name"_t2.as<"first_name">(), 
      "last_name"_t2.as<"last_name">(), 
      "salary"_t2.as<"salary">()
  )
  | sqlgen::full_join<Department>("department_id"_t2 == "department_id"_t1);

  // Execute the queries
  auto inner_results = inner_query(conn).value();
  auto left_results = left_query(conn).value();
  auto right_results = right_query(conn).value();
  auto full_results = full_query(conn).value();

  // Return the analysis
  return JoinAnalysis{
      .inner_join_results = inner_results,
      .left_join_results = left_results,
      .right_join_results = right_results,
      .full_join_results = full_results
  };
}

}  // namespace analyze_department_employees

}

}  // namespace create_view_order_summary

    #pragma once

    #include <sqlgen.hpp>
    #include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
    #include <vector>

    namespace project_time_tracking {

    struct Project {
      sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> project_id;
      std::string name;
    };

    struct TimeLog {
      sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> log_id;
      int project_id;
      sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> start_time;
      sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> end_time;
    };

    struct TimeMetrics {
      sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> project_id;
      std::string name;
      int day;
      int month;
      int year;
      int hour;
      int minute;
      int second;
      double duration_days;
    };

    std::vector<TimeMetrics> project_time_tracking() {
      using namespace sqlgen::literals;

      // Build the query
      auto query = select_from<Project, "p">(
          sqlgen::Col<"project_id", "p">{},
          sqlgen::Col<"name", "p">{},
          day(sqlgen::Col<"start_time", "t">{}).as<"day">(),
          month(sqlgen::Col<"start_time", "t">{}).as<"month">(),
          year(sqlgen::Col<"start_time", "t">{}).as<"year">(),
          hour(sqlgen::Col<"start_time", "t">{}).as<"hour">(),
          minute(sqlgen::Col<"start_time", "t">{}).as<"minute">(),
          second(sqlgen::Col<"start_time", "t">{}).as<"second">(),
          days_between(sqlgen::Col<"start_time", "t">{}, sqlgen::Col<"end_time", "t">{}).as<"duration_days">()
      ) | inner_join<TimeLog, "t">(sqlgen::Col<"project_id", "p">{} == sqlgen::Col<"project_id", "t">{}) | to<std::vector<TimeMetrics>>;

      // Connect to the database
      auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
      if (!conn_result.is_ok()) {
        return std::vector<TimeMetrics>{};
      }
      auto conn = conn_result.value();

      // Execute the query
      auto result = query(conn);
      if (result.is_ok()) {
        return result.value();
      } else {
        return std::vector<TimeMetrics>{};
      }

#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace project_time_tracking {

struct Project {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> project_id;
  std::string name;
};

struct TimeLog {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> log_id;
  int project_id;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> start_time;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> end_time;
};

struct TimeMetrics {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> project_id;
  std::string name;
  int day;
  int month;
  int year;
  int hour;
  int minute;
  int second;
  double duration_days;
};

std::vector<TimeMetrics> project_time_tracking() {
  using namespace sqlgen;
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;

  // Build the query with join and field extraction
  auto query = select_from<Project, "p">(
      Col<"project_id", "p">{},
      Col<"name", "p">{},
      day(Col<"start_time", "t">{}).as<"day">(),
      month(Col<"start_time", "t">{}).as<"month">(),
      year(Col<"start_time", "t">{}).as<"year">(),
      hour(Col<"start_time", "t">{}).as<"hour">(),
      minute(Col<"start_time", "t">{}).as<"minute">(),
      second(Col<"start_time", "t">{}).as<"second">(),
      days_between(Col<"start_time", "t">{}, Col<"end_time", "t">{}).as<"duration_days">()
  ) | inner_join<TimeLog, "t">(Col<"project_id", "p">{} == Col<"project_id", "t">{}) 
    | to<std::vector<TimeMetrics>>;

  // Connect to database and execute query
  auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  if (!conn_result.is_ok()) {
    return std::vector<TimeMetrics>{};
  }
  auto conn = conn_result.value();

  auto result = query(conn);
  if (result.is_ok()) {
    return result.value();
  } else {
    return std::vector<TimeMetrics>{};
  }
}

}  // namespace project_time_tracking
